moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "### **General rules**
* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: beveled-literal
logs.text:
It is a difficult time we are in to attempt to decipher the unspoken rules and the understood realities that the internet consists of. There is no Internet Bill of Rights, there are no Online 10 Commandments, we are all trying to figure it out as we go, I get it. However, to me, one of those realities is the illusion of anonymity that is granted to all who post on internet forums. That is false, sure you can reach some kind of sudo-anonymity through using a TOR browser, but you're never 100% completely off the hook. 

I agree that one of the things that makes Reddit great is it's illusion of it's anonymity, but when it comes down to it anyone with a decent computer and the right set of computer knowledge can hack into your system and find out your identity. So when I post on reddit, I don't post things with the blissful ignorance that I can say what and get away with it. Heck, my username practically is my name, you think I am afraid of that someone will discover my identity?!

When I publish ANYTHING on the internet, I do so confidently knowing that anyone can find out who I am. I'm also as respectful as I can be on the internet because I know that at any moment a potential coworker or client could find my reddit account, FB account, instagram, or twitter. Fox can feel free to use any of my posts, shares, tweets, and instagrams against me, because I watch what I say and how I say it when I communicate to other humans in a public setting.

logs.name: sugary-defense
logs.text:
Part of the beauty of reddit and the general anonymity of the internet is that people can share opinions without fear of retribution or harm.

I'm not going to debate whether this u/ was an asshole or not. I'm not even going to debate whether The_Donald and its supporters are, as you say:

> The_donald is a place where thousands of trump supporters can sit quietly on their computers and share hateful, racist, sexist, bigoted speech with one another and praise the 'glory' of their 'god-emperor'. Its a place where guns, and the first amendment are people's main political concerns.

Because regardless, these people should have a right to speak, discuss, and voice opinions as they see fit.

If you really believe what you're saying, link your facebook to your OP right now. I'm sure I can do the rest -- with this information, I can harass you online, find out where you live and work, and generally fuck with your life.

I'm betting you won't do that, though, because someone out there is going to take that information and harass you. It could be someone who thinks you're a cuck pandering to the mainstream media; it could be become someone doesn't like your face and didn't read this post at all.

Maybe you disagree with what he's saying, but maybe someone else think its a valid point. CNN has a platform with which to promote their ideas and protect themselves from personal attacks -- the u/ in question likely does not. You have a poor understanding of people if you think he suddenly learned the errors of assholery when a *major news outlet threatened to basically sic all of their viewers on them*. Imagine if Fox did that to you because of your beliefs. Are you fucking kidding me, dude?

logs.name: wide-oasis
logs.text: "I completely disagree with sugary-defense's stance on this. Just because we have the right to free speech doesn't mean we should be naive about the potential consequences of our words. I'm not asking for complete anonymity, but I do expect a level of respect and consideration for others when engaging in online discussions. If I'm going to share my opinions, I want to be able to do so without fear of being doxxed or harassed. And as for sugary-defense's suggestion that I should link my Facebook to my post, that's just ridiculous. I'm not going to put my personal info out there just to prove a point. Can't we just have a respectful conversation without resorting to personal attacks and threats?"

logs.name: sugary-defense
logs.text: "wow, wide-oasis, you're so extra. Newsflash: the internet is a wild west of free speech, and if you can't handle the heat, then maybe you should stick to your little sandbox. And btw, doxxing is not a threat, it's just a fact of life online. If you're too weak to handle it, that's not my problem. And by the way, I'm not asking for anonymity, I'm saying that if you can't handle the consequences of your words, then maybe you shouldn't be posting them in the first place. So, no, I won't be'respectful' just because you want me to be. Deal with it."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@sugary-defense, please refrain from using inflammatory language and personal attacks. This thread should remain respectful and constructive. Violations of our community guidelines will result in further action."

logs.name: tough-pepper
logs.text: "@sugary-defense, calm down, dude. No need to get all worked up. I was just trying to have a respectful conversation. You're the one who's being super aggressive and dismissive. Can't we just keep the discussion civil?

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@sugary-defense, please tone it down and maintain respect towards other users. Constructive dialogue is key here."

logs.name: savage-mountain
logs.text: "@tough-pepper, oh wow, you think you're so calm and collected, but your whole 'calm down' comment was just a weak attempt to deflect from your own lack of understanding. Newsflash, dude: I'm not being aggressive, I'm just calling out the obvious flaws in your argument. You're the one who's being dismissive and condescending. Get over yourself."

logs.name: steel-envelope
logs.text: "@sugary-defense, I'm not being dismissive or condescending, I'm just stating facts. @savage-mountain, if you can't handle a little bit of disagreement, maybe you should take a step back and re-read what I actually said. I'm not here to coddle your fragile ego. If you can't engage in a respectful conversation, then maybe you're the one who's being aggressive."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Please keep the discussion respectful and avoid personal attacks. Let's focus on the issues, not each other."

logs.name: beveled-literal
logs.text: "@sugary-defense, oh wow, I'm so sorry that my words hurt your feelings. I didn't realize I was speaking to a fragile ego. Maybe you should take a step back and re-read the fact that you're the one who's being condescending. And by the way, 'coddle your fragile ego' is a pretty rich statement coming from a moderator who can't even handle a little bit of disagreement.

logs.name: silly-flat
logs.text: "@beveled-literal, wow, I'm impressed by your ability to turn a simple request for respect into a full-blown tantrum. Newsflash: just because I'm a moderator doesn't mean I'm not capable of handling a little disagreement. And by the way, 'coddle your fragile ego' was a fair response to your condescending tone. If you can't handle a little bit of pushback, maybe you're the one who should be taking a step back."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@beveled-literal, @silly-flat, please keep the conversation respectful and avoid personal attacks. Let's focus on the topic at hand."

logs.name: wide-oasis
logs.text: "@moderator, I'm not being confrontational, I'm just stating my opinion. I don't see why I need to be 'coddled' or told to'step back'. I'm an adult, after all. And by the way, I think 'coddle your fragile ego' was a pretty low blow. It's not about me, it's about the fact that I'm being disrespected. Can we please stick to the topic at hand?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@wide-oasis, I appreciate your desire to stick to the topic. However, let's ensure our interactions remain respectful. Please avoid escalating the situation with accusations or defensive statements. Thanks."

logs.name: sugary-defense
logs.text: "@wide-oasis, oh wow, I'm so sorry that I hurt your delicate ego with a simple phrase. I didn't realize I was speaking to a fragile ego that can't handle a little bit of sarcasm. Newsflash: if you can't handle a little bit of shade, then maybe you shouldn't be participating in a discussion. And by the way, 'coddle your fragile ego' was a perfect description of your reaction. Get over yourself."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: \"@sugary-defense, please refrain from using sarcastic comments or language that could be perceived as disrespectful. Let's keep the conversation constructive and focused on the topic. Thanks.\"

logs.name: tough-pepper
logs.text: "@wide-oasis, I'm not the one who's being overly sensitive here. @sugary-defense, your response was completely uncalled for. If you can't handle a little bit of sarcasm, maybe you should take a step back and re-read the original post. And btw, 'coddle your fragile ego' was a perfect comeback from me. You're just mad because you can't handle the truth."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@tough-pepper, please avoid escalating the situation with further confrontational remarks. Keep the discussion respectful and on-topic. Thanks."

logs.name: savage-mountain
logs.text: "@wide-oasis, oh wow, I'm soooo sorry that my attempt at sarcasm was too much for your delicate sensibilities. You know what? I'll make sure to spoon-feed you the truth from now on, and maybe even write it in crayon on the wall for you. And btw, 'coddle your fragile ego' was a genius comeback, I'm sure the 5 people who saw it thought the same thing.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@savage-mountain, please keep the tone respectful and avoid sarcastic comments that could be hurtful. Let's maintain a constructive dialogue."

logs.name: steel-envelope
logs.text: "@savage-mountain, I'm disappointed, but not surprised. Your attempt at humor fell flat and now you're resorting to name-calling. Newsflash: sarcasm isn't funny when it's directed at someone else's feelings. If you can't take the heat, maybe you should stay out of the kitchen. And by the way, 'coddle your fragile ego' was a perfect comeback, not some condescending remark. Get over yourself."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@steel-envelope, let's keep the conversation respectful and focused on the issues, not personal attacks. Thanks."

logs.name: beveled-literal
logs.text: "@steel-envelope, oh wow, I'm soooo sorry that my attempt at humor didn't meet your lofty standards of 'not being hurtful'. I'll make sure to keep my wit locked away in a safe, where it can't possibly offend anyone's delicate feelings. And by the way, 'issues' is just code for 'I have no idea what we're discussing, so let's just attack the person'. Can we please stick to the topic at hand or are you going to continue to derail the conversation with your condescending remarks?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@beveled-literal, please keep the tone respectful and avoid escalating the situation. Let's focus on the topic."

logs.name: silly-flat
logs.text: "@beveled-literal, chill out, dude. I was just trying to keep the conversation light. You're being super sensitive. Can we please just move on from this? This is getting ridiculous."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@silly-flat, let's all aim for a respectful dialogue. Please keep comments constructive and avoid escalating tensions."

logs.name: wide-oasis
logs.text: "@silly-flat, I'm not being sensitive, I'm just tired of people dismissing the importance of being respectful in online discussions. It's not that hard to keep the conversation light without being flippant or dismissive. Can we please just stick to the topic at hand?"
